name: Virtuals Protocol
description: AI agent launchpad on Base (Ethereum L2). No-code platform to create, tokenize, and deploy AI agents across DeFi, gaming, and social media.
researchNotes:
Research conducted April 2026. Sources: Yahoo Finance, CoinTelegraph, CoinBureau, CoinGecko, Bitstamp Learn, CoinSpot, 99Bitcoins, Virtuals Protocol whitepaper.

Key facts:
- Launched October 2024 on Base (Ethereum L2)
- Ecosystem exceeded $1B total value
- Notable agents include aixbt (500K followers), LUNA, and others
- BASISO FRAUD (November 25, 2025): BasisOS, a supposed AI yield optimization agent, stole $500K of user funds. Was actually operated entirely by a human mimicking automated behavior. Ran for weeks undetected. Virtuals co-founders had promoted the BIOS token. Co-founder committed to reimbursing affected users.
- Post-BasisOS: purged non-verified agents, required on-chain decision logging, implemented verification system
- Earlier Genesis launch model had quality issues: 'fairness turned into farming,' farmers rotating between launches, quality teams stayed away
- CoinBureau 2026 review: 'profitable agent ecosystem has yet to be realized'
- VIRTUAL token surged ~20% post-BasisOS incident after verification changes
- Verified agents saw increased confidence after the purge

evidence.Agent creation and deployment: No-code interface works. Ecosystem grew to $1B+ since Oct 2024. Produced notable agents (aixbt, LUNA). Low barrier is both strength and weakness: many low-quality agents created.
evidence.Tokenized agent ownership: Bonding curve mechanics work on-chain, verifiable via BaseScan. Creator allocations and fees match whitepaper. BUT tokenization creates perverse incentives: BasisOS was tokenized and traded while being a complete fraud.
evidence.Cross-platform agent operation: Multiple agents (aixbt, LUNA) operate across X, Discord, Telegram. Persona consistency maintained. Platform-appropriate formatting works.
evidence.Agent revenue distribution: Revenue sources exist: trading fees, inference fees, platform fees. On-chain fees verifiable. BUT off-chain metrics (API usage) not independently auditable. CoinBureau: 'profitable agent ecosystem has yet to be realized.'
evidence.Agent quality assurance: CRITICAL FAILURE. BasisOS: human posing as AI agent for weeks, promoted by Virtuals co-founders, stole $500K. Genesis launch model had farming problems. Post-BasisOS: verification requirements added, non-verified agents purged, on-chain logging required. Co-founder committed to reimbursing BasisOS victims.
